item.title: Here’s How The New Resident Evil Movie Fits Into The Games’ Timeline
item.content:encoded:
This morning, Sony released the first trailer for its upcoming Resident Evil movie, which isn’t an adaptation of any of the games. In fact, we don’t see much of anything that seems to directly connect the film to the stories of Leon Kennedy, Chris Redfield, or the Umbrella corporation in this first clip. However, director Zach Cregger sat down with IGN to talk about the trailer, and revealed how the film will be connected to the broader Resident Evil timeline when it comes to theaters in September.

The 13-minute trailer breakdown shows Weapons director Cregger talking about some of the shots they showed in the trailer, his personal Resident Evil history, and working with Capcom on the horror series’ next live-action film. You can watch the full thing below, but one of the first things he talks about is how the new movie will tie into the Resident Evil games. The film stars Austin Abrams as Bryan, a medical courier who has his own problems to deal with while Leon and co. are out trying to save the world. According to Cregger, this movie is meant to take place parallel to the events of Resident Evil 2 and the outbreak in Raccoon City.

“Basically my rule for this movie was to just try and try and make a movie that felt like my experience of playing the game that could live in the world of Resident Evil,” Cregger tells IGN. “I feel like this movie takes place alongside the events of Resident Evil 2. I like to think that everything that’s going on in the police station could be happening in this world. This is just another dude on another mission on the other side of town and like what’s going on with him.”

It remains to be seen just how overt the references to Raccoon City and Leon’s first day as a police officer will be, but it does sound like the movie’s sense of time and place are pretty concrete. Cregger says that Sony and Capcom and the powers that be didn’t interfere with his vision of a movie that wasn’t a more direct franchise tie-in, so some of the references are pretty subtle, such as a Green Herb pot from Resident Evil 4 that’s visible in the background of one of the shots in the trailer.

“I was very, very fortunate to have a studio and a production company that really supported me and never got in the way [with] ‘Well, you know, we got to make sure we’re doing X, Y, or Z,’” he says. “I was given nothing but support. I know there’s all kinds of horror stories you hear about filmmakers dabbling with IP and running into all sorts of frustrations, but I truly didn’t have that.”

Resident Evil is coming to theaters on September 18.



https://kotaku.com/resident-evil-movie-canon-zach-cregger-raccoon-city-2000692100

item.title: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Surpasses 1 Million Wishlists
item.content:encoded:
Ubisoft has revealed that Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced, the upcoming remake, has surpassed one million wishlists across all platforms.

It was only a week ago that the game was officially unveiled to the public during the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Showcase, meaning it’s garnered over a million wishlists in seven days. Based on the hype surrounding the reveal, as high as the number might be, it’s not entirely surprising.

Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Due July 9, 2026

Insider Gaming’s own Tom Henderson broke the news that Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced would be releasing on July 9, 2026, with the date being reaffirmed during the April showcase.

It’s not just going to be a simple remaster of the fan-favourite title, either, as there are plenty of changes coming alongside the remake. Perhaps most notably, the combat has been reworked to depict an action-oriented experience, whilst the frustrating tailing missions have been greatly improved.

And don’t worry, if you weren’t a fan of the more modern, RPG Assassin’s Creed titles, you’ll be glad to hear that the Black Flag remake won’t be following in their footsteps. Ubisoft were clear during the reveal that it will not be an RPG, as the game maintains its original character-driven experience.

If you were one of the million people who decided to wishlist the game, there’s a chance you opted for the Collector’s Edition, which includes an impressive Edward Kenway statue, along with a host of other goodies.

With only a few months left to go, Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced will soon be playable, and it’s obvious there’s a serious amount of hype surrounding the title.

Have you wishlisted Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, check out the first trailer for the Resident Evil movie, and read about Take-Two not committing to a GTA 6 price amid $100 rumors.

item.title: Black Desert Online Opens Arena of Solare Season 9, With Major Improvements, Adds New Node War Territories
item.content:encoded:
Black Desert Online’s latest update begins Arena of Solare Season 9, with a series of improvements to the 3v3 mode, including reworked rewards. The team also added new Node Wars territories.

Arena of Solare operates with normalized gear, so it already starts with an effort to keep things  on equal footing, making skill the determining factor.  Season 9's updates, Pearl Abyss says,  are designed to make combat better, more enjoyable, and more accessible for all skill levels.  One big change opens the battlefield rotation to six environments. Gyfin Rhasia Temple (Upper), Kermelun Wilds, Neftak Outpost, and Olun’s Valley all return, and Darkseeker’s Retreat and Yolu’s Teeth are the two new arenas. Expects Variety in the environments as well as new tactical opportunities. 

In season 9, the ranking and point systems have been reworked for transparency and reduced friction. Base point gains and losses will scale by rank, and the top Solare tier requires a qualifying score and a top 30 ranking to enter. This change is because previously, the gain / loss rate was almost uniform on all tiers, which made it really difficult to get ahead 

The team has also reworked the Arena of Solare rewards. Now, both Victory and defeat reward boxes will offer more gold bars and Seals of Solare, with an expanded Seal exchange. Those changes are intended to offer more valuable and more practical items.

All of these changes, the team promises to keep a close look on everything, especially to look out for potential balance issues. They plan to examine all classes and keep an eye on things as the season progresses. If they need to make any significant changes for balance reasons, they're also planning to do that earlier in the season.

As far as other changes, there are two new Node War territories: Ulukita and Edania. With the addition of these new battlefields, they will reorganize the existing territory groups and rotate Tier 1 and tier 3 territories. Going forward, they're going to rotate regularly to offer a reliable variety of options. 

You can check out the full patch notes for stats and info on all of these changes before you jump in.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/black-desert-online-opens-arena-of-solare-season-9-with-major-improvements-adds-new-node-war-territories-2000137945
